title: Dugandiodendron ekmanii
taxonomy: Dugandiodendron ekmanii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the order Magnoliales and the family Annonaceae. Within the family Annonaceae, Dugandiodendron ekmanii is further classified under the subfamily Annonoideae and the tribe Malmeeae. At the genus level, it is part of the monotypic genus Dugandiodendron. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.

The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various infections and diseases. The bioactive constituents of Dugandiodendron ekmanii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
